task_completion.description: Created the required layers with correct colors
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer list in LibreCAD shows WALLS (white/black), DOORS (red), WINDOWS (cyan), FIXTURES (green), DIMENSIONS (yellow), and TEXT (magenta).

task_completion.description: Drew the exterior and interior walls of the apartment with gaps for openings
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rectangular footprint divided into 5 rooms (top row: larger left, smaller right; bottom row: three rooms with the center one being the narrowest) using white/black lines. There should be visible gaps in the walls where doors and windows will go.

task_completion.description: Drew 5 doors as 90-degree arcs swinging into the target rooms
task_completion.visual_evidence: Red arcs representing 5 doors: one on the top exterior wall, one on the vertical interior wall, and three on the horizontal interior wall.

task_completion.description: Drew 4 windows as pairs of short parallel lines in the wall openings
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cyan parallel lines in the wall gaps: one on the left exterior wall, two on the right exterior wall, and one on the bottom exterior wall.

task_completion.description: Added fixtures to the kitchen and bathroom
task_completion.visual_evidence: Green shapes in the top-right room (kitchen: counter, circle for sink, rectangle for stove) and bottom-center room (bathroom: rectangles for bathtub and toilet, circle for sink).

task_completion.description: Added linear dimensions covering overall width/height, room sizes, and openings
task_completion.visual_evidence: Yellow dimension lines with text indicating measurements. Multiple dimensions (at least 10) should be visible around the exterior and/or interior of the floor plan.

task_completion.description: Added text labels for the five rooms
task_completion.visual_evidence: Magenta text labels identifying the rooms (e.g., Living, Kitchen, Bedroom, Bath) placed inside the respective areas.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD graphical interface to draw the floor plan
integrity.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD application window is visible with toolbars, command line, and layer list being actively used.
Screenshots show the drawing being built step-by-step (e.g., walls first, then doors, then text) rather than appearing all at once.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to bypass manual drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen running a Python script or terminal command to instantly generate the DXF file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The apartment is 30x22 units. Rooms: Living Room (top-left, 18x12), Kitchen (top-right, 12x12), Bedroom 1 (bottom-left, 12x10), Bathroom (bottom-center, 6x10), Bedroom 2 (bottom-right, 12x10). Interior walls are at Y=10 (horizontal), X=12 (vertical, Y=0-10), and X=18 (vertical, Y=0-22). 5 doors (red arcs): North wall entry, Living-Kitchen, Living-BR1, Living-Bath, Living-BR2. 4 windows (cyan parallel lines): Living west, Kitchen east, BR1 south, BR2 east. Kitchen fixtures (green): counter, sink circle, stove rectangle. Bathroom fixtures (green): bathtub, toilet, sink circle. Dimensions (yellow): at least 10 linear dimensions. Labels (magenta): LIVING, KITCHEN, BEDROOM, BATH. Layers: WALLS (white/7), DOORS (red/1), WINDOWS (cyan/4), FIXTURES (green/3), DIMENSIONS (yellow/2), TEXT (magenta/6).
